info.title: Phare
info.description: Learn how to use the phare.io API. Most of the things that can be done on the web platform can also be achieved with the API documented on this page.

get.description: Get basic API information
Get a paginated list of alert rules.

project_id.description: The ID of a project to use as a scope

integration_id.description: The ID of the integration used to send a notification when the alert rule is executed

rate_limit.description: Minimum time in minutes between two executions of the alert rule, used to minimized the number of alert received.

updated_at.description: Date of last update for the entity

get.description: Get a paginated list of monitors

paused.description: Whether the monitor is currently paused

response_time.description: Rolling average response time of the last 10 requests, in milliseconds

request.description: Monitoring request, depends of the chosen protocol

body.description: HTTP request body for POST, PUT and PATCH requests

user_agent_secret.description: A secret value to be used in the User-Agent header for authenticating the request

headers.description: Additional HTTP headers to add to the request

value.description: Header value

host.description: TCP request hostname or IP address

success_assertions.description: List of assertions that must be true for the check to be considered successful

operator.description: Operator to use for the assertion

value.description: A comma-separated list of status code values, you can use x as a wildcard for any digit.

selector.description: The name of the header to assert

operator.description: Operator to use for the assertion

value.description: The value of the header to assert

operator.description: Operator to use for the assertion

value.description: A word or sentence to check in the response body

incident_confirmations.description: Number of uninterrupted failed checks required to create an incident

recovery_confirmations.description: Number of uninterrupted successful checks required to resolve an incident

region_threshold.description: Number of regions that must fail before an incident is confirmed

regions.description: List of regions where monitoring checks are performed

get.description: Get uptime statistics for a single monitor by ID in the selected time window

parameters.name: monitorId
parameters.description: ID of the monitor to report on

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start of the reporting window

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End of the reporting window

incident_count.description: Number of incidents affecting this monitor in the selected range

downtime.description: Total downtime for this monitor in seconds

availability.description: Availability for this monitor in the selected range, as a percentage

mttr.description: Mean time to recovery (MTTR) for this monitor, in seconds

mtbf.description: Mean time between failures (MTBF) for this monitor, in seconds

get.description: Get aggregated uptime statistics for the selected time window

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start of the reporting window

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End of the reporting window

downtime.description: Total downtime across all monitors in seconds

availability.description: Mean monitor availability across the selected range, as a percentage

min_availability.description: Lowest monitor availability in the selected range, as a percentage

max_availability.description: Highest monitor availability in the selected range, as a percentage

mttr.description: Mean time to recovery (MTTR) average for all monitors, in seconds

mtbf.description: Mean time between failures (MTBF) average for all monitors, in seconds

report_start_at.description: Effective start of the generated report window

report_end_at.description: Effective end of the generated report window

monitors.description: Per-monitor statistics for all monitors included in the report

get.description: Get a paginated list of status pages

is_private.description: Whether the status page requires authentication to access

access_password_enabled.description: Whether the status page is protected by a password

access_token_enabled.description: Whether the status page is protected by a secret token.

subdomain.description: Subdomain name for the status page, hosted on a phare.io domain: {subdomain}.status.phare.io

domain.description: Custom domain name for the status page

search_engine_indexed.description: Whether search engines are allowed to index the page

website_url.description: URL to redirect users clicking the status page logo

color_scheme.description: Choose the available color schemes for the status page

theme.description: Theme settings to customize the status page

components.description: List of components to show on the status page

componentable_id.description: ID of the monitor to show

timeframe.description: Number of days of status and incident history to display.

access_ips.description: List of IP addresses or CIDR ranges allowed to access the status page. When null, IP restriction is disabled.

logo_light.description: Manage the light theme logo of the status page (accepted formats: jpeg / png / svg)

oneOf.description: Any image file with the following format: jpeg / png / svg.
If "false" is passed to the request the logo will be removed

logo_dark.description: Manage the dark theme logo of the status page (accepted formats: jpeg / png / svg)

oneOf.description: Any image file with the following format: jpeg / png / svg.
If "false" is passed to the request the logo will be removed

favicon_light.description: Manage the light theme favicon of the status page (accepted formats: png / svg)

oneOf.description: Any image file with the following format: png / svg.
If "false" is passed to the request the favicon will be removed

favicon_dark.description: Manage the dark theme favicon of the status page (accepted formats: png / svg)

oneOf.description: Any image file with the following format: png / svg.
If "false" is passed to the request the favicon will be removed

active_incidents.description: Active incidents linked to the status page

message.example: The platform:write permission is required to perform this action.

header_project_id.name: X-Phare-Project-Id
header_project_id.description: A project header is required when using an organization-scoped API key.

header_project_slug.name: X-Phare-Project-Slug
header_project_slug.description: A project header is required when using an organization-scoped API key.

per_page.name: per_page
per_page.description: Number of resources to return per page

BearerAuth.description: Use a user token to access authenticated routes. The token must be specified in the Authorization HTTP header with the following format 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'.
